title: narcissus tazetta var. canariensis
intro: Narcissus tazetta var. canariensis, also known as the Canary Island narcissus or grape hyacinth, is a type of bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to be used as an anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Narcissus tazetta var. canariensis include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of this plant include its use in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant.
